# PAI

> PAI is a bootable USB Linux distribution for private, offline AI. It is a
> Debian 12 live image with Sway, Ollama, and Open WebUI preinstalled. Users
> flash the ISO to a USB stick (or flash the arm64 image to a Raspberry Pi SD
> card via Raspberry Pi Imager using PAI's custom repository at
> `https://pai.direct/imager.json`), boot the target machine from it, and
> land in a self-contained AI workstation that runs entirely on-device — no
> installation, no cloud account, no telemetry. Pull the stick out to return
> to the host OS.

PAI targets amd64 and arm64 hardware and is licensed under GPL v3. The source
of truth for the project is <https://github.com/nirholas/pai>. ISO artifacts
are hosted on Google Cloud Storage because they exceed GitHub's per-asset
size limit.

## Getting started

- [Quickstart](/quickstart.md): flash a USB stick and boot PAI in about a minute
- [Installation](/installation.md): supported hardware and boot requirements
- [USB flashing](/USB-FLASHING.md): per-OS flashing guides (Linux, macOS, Windows — `flash.ps1` PowerShell one-liner recommended on Windows; Rufus is a graphical alternative)
- [Raspberry Pi Imager](/first-steps/using-raspberry-pi-imager.md): install PAI on a Pi via Raspberry Pi Imager's custom repository (`https://pai.direct/imager.json`)
- [Using Ventoy](/first-steps/using-ventoy.md): boot PAI from a Ventoy multi-boot USB without re-flashing — drag-and-drop the ISO onto a Ventoy drive
- [Using PAI](/using-pai.md): the desktop, apps, and day-to-day workflow

## Privacy and security

- [Privacy posture](/PRIVACY.md): what PAI does and does not send over the network
- [Privacy mode](/privacy-mode.md): optional Tor routing and hardening toggles
- [Security model](/security.md): threat model, firewall defaults, MAC randomization
- [Persistence](/persistence.md): how opt-in encrypted persistence works (roadmap)

## Reference

- [Architecture](/architecture.md): how the ISO is built and what's on it
- [Models](/models.md): which LLMs ship on the ISO and how to pull more
- [FAQ](/FAQ.md)
- [Known issues](/KNOWN_ISSUES.md)
- [Glossary](/GLOSSARY.md)
